Scientists discover new frog species small enough to sit on a fingernail Scientists discover new frog species small enough to sit on a fingernail Scientists discover new frog species small enough to sit on a fingernail The newly discovered frog species is exceptionally small, even by frog standards. Adult specimens measure only about 10 millimeters in length, making it one of the smallest frog species in the world. To put this into perspective, it is smaller than a dime and can easily fit on the tip of a human finger. When compared to other known frog species, which can range from a few millimeters to over 30 centimeters in length, this tiny frog is a remarkable discovery that highlights the vast biodiversity in nature.

Were fragrances to blame? Chronic coughing, defined as a cough that lasts for more than 8 weeks, can be caused by a variety of factors. Some of the most common causes include postnasal drip, asthma, gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), chronic bronchitis, and certain medications, such as angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitors. Exposure to fragrances can also trigger coughing in individuals who are sensitive or allergic to certain chemicals found in fragrances.
